nyheter.summary:
A U.S. Navy drone task force picked up two downed pilots using a Saronic Corsair unmanned surface vessel (USV) yesterday in Middle Eastern waters during a first of its kind rescue mission. nyheter.title: German F125 frigates are to receive 32 Iris-T SLM Naval missiles
nyheter.summary:
The German Navy’s F125 (Baden-Württemberg-class) frigates, which have so far been extremely poorly armed in terms of air defense, are to receive a significant capability upgrade in the future. The defense contractor Diehl Defence is currently working on equipping the ships with a total of 32 Iris-T-SLM missile cells. By Lars Hoffman / hartpunkt According ...

nyheter.summary: Four Navy flag officers have been nominated for three-star assignments, according to a Monday Pentagon announcement. Vice Adm. Scott Gray, current commander of Navy Installations Command, has been renominated for a third star to be the deputy chief of Naval Operations for Sustainment, N4, Office of the Chief of Naval Operations/commander, Navy Sustainment, according to the announcement. nyheter.title: Philippines commissions new OPV BRP Rajah Lakandula
nyheter.summary:
The Philippine Navy on June 9, 2026, welcomed the offshore patrol vessel (OPV) BRP Rajah Lakandula (PS-21) into active service. It is the second Sulayman-class OPV built by HD HHI in South Korea. President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. led the commissioning ceremony as part of the Navy’s 128th anniversary celebration. The event also marked the first ...

On May 18, 2026, the Yomiuri Shimbun, one of Japan’s leading national newspapers, reported that the Japan Self-Defense Forces (JSDF) are considering equipping unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) with airborne early warning (AEW) radar systems. nyheter.title: Norfolk Sailor Pleads Guilty to Murder of Fellow Sailor
nyheter.summary: NAVAL STATION NORFOLK, Va. — A Norfolk sailor pleaded guilty Monday to murdering a fellow sailor in May 2025 as part of a plea agreement that will see him sentenced to a minimum of 40 years in a federal prison. Culinary Specialist 3rd Class Jeremiah Copeland appeared before a military judge for a general court-martial and sentencing in a courtroom across the street from the barracks in which he admitted to murdering CS3 Angelina Resendiz. nyheter.title: Chinese Flotilla Surges East of Taiwan Amid Spat with the Philippines, Japan
nyheter.summary: China deployed six cutters and patrol ships east of the Luzon Strait in its second “special maritime law enforcement operation” following a diplomatic row with the Philippines and Japan. On Saturday, China’s Ministry of Transport led a mission involving the Fujian and Guangdong regional maritime security administrations east of Taiwan. nyheter.summary: This post has been identified with the sailor who died on Saturday. One sailor is dead and a second sailor is in pre-trial confinement after an early morning Saturday shooting aboard carrier John F. Kennedy (CVN-79), USNI News has learned. Boatswain’s Mate Seaman Jesse Braswell of Colorado was killed in the shooting, which happened around 5:15 a.m. on Saturday, a Navy spokesperson told USNI News. “Our deepest condolences are with [Seaman] Braswell’s family, friends and shipmates. STM, Türkiye’s leading naval engineering company, has launched RAJA LAUT, the second vessel of the three-corvette project being built for the Royal Malaysian Navy, during a ceremony held in Istanbul. Following the launch of the first vessel two weeks ago, the project continues on track, with the third ship scheduled to be launched in August. ...

nyheter.title: U.S. Forces Interdict Ship Connected to Iran in Indian Ocean, Iranian Oil Exports See Decrease
nyheter.summary: U.S. Indo-Pacific Command forces interdicted and boarded a stateless vessel in the Indian Ocean late Thursday as part of the expanded U.S. blockade of Iranian ports, the combatant command posted on social media site X. The INDOPACOM post did not indicate whether the U.S. took possession of MT Davina (IMO: 9259367), which has gone by other names, including MT Lenore. The ship sails under a false Curacaoan flag, according to the International Maritime Organization. The U.S. has sanctioned the ship

nyheter.title: House Authorizers Adopt Amendment for Navy Battleship Study
nyheter.summary: House authorizers want a Navy study that assesses how pursuing the nuclear-powered Trump-class battleship program could affect the shipbuilding and nuclear industrial base. The House Armed Services Committee adopted an amendment from Rep. Joe Courtney (D-Conn.) that would require both the Secretary of the Navy and the admiral leading the Naval Nuclear Propulsion Program to submit a report by March explaining the acquisition plans for the BBG(X) and how the service will avoid negative impacts to

nyheter.title: Leaders of U.S. Navy’s Ship Maintenance Facility in Japan Removed from Command
nyheter.summary: The leadership of the Navy’s primary ship maintenance facility in Japan was removed from command this week, the service announced Wednesday. U.S. Naval Ship Repair Facility and Japan Regional Maintenance Center (SRF-JRMC) commander Capt. Wendel Penetrante, executive officer Capt. Edwin Catubig and Master Chief Petty Officer Thomas Howell were removed from their positions “due to a loss of confidence in their ability to command” by Navy Regional Maintenance Center commander Rear Adm. Dan Lannaman

nyheter.title: Task Force Ashland Returns to California from 4-month Indo-Pacific Deployment
nyheter.summary: Specialized Marine Air-Ground Task Force Ashland recently completed a four-month deployment to the Indo-Pacific, returning to California in the past week. The Marine Corps element of TF Ashland returned Friday to Camp Pendleton, disembarking from amphibious dock landing ship USS Ashland (LSD-48). The ship arrived Monday at its homeport of San Diego, just over four months after it departed Jan. 24. TF Ashland was comprised of Ashland and its crew, a command element from the 15th Marine Expedition

nyheter.title: Philippines Progresses Japanese Destroyer Transfer Talks, Receives American Cutter at Shangri-La
nyheter.summary: The Philippines is set to receive transfers of American and Japanese vessels to bolster its Coast Guard and Navy fleets operating in the South China Sea. American and Japanese defense officials pledged to reinforce Manila’s fleet with recently and soon-to-be decommissioned vessels over the weekend following talks on the sidelines of the International Institute of Strategic Studies Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore held Friday to Sunday. nyheter.title: French Navy Seizes Sanctioned Russian Tanker, 4th Since September
nyheter.summary: The French Navy seized sanctioned tanker MT Tagor on Sunday while it operated in the Atlantic, marking the service’s fourth shadow fleet seizure since September. The tanker was seized on suspicion of flying a false Cameroonian flag and diverted to France, arriving Tuesday in Douarnenez Bay where the ship is detained, the Maritime Prefecture of the Atlantic announced Monday. rov_teknologi.summary: The Pentagon’s planned energy depot in the Philippines will be able to refuel the U.S. Navy’s guided-missile destroyers and Combat Logistics Force ships, according to new documents. Amendments to the Defense Logistics Agency’s solicitation from last summer have expanded, requiring the bidder to provide fueling support for Arleigh Burke-class destroyers, fleet replenishment oilers, dry cargo vessels and fast combat support ships. rov_teknologi.summary:

rov_teknologi.title: Navy Pilot Programs Aim to Expand Sailor Food Options At Galleys
rov_teknologi.summary: Sailors stationed at Naval Base Kitsap, Wash., and Naval Construction Battalion Center Gulfport, Miss., will now have access to a wider variety of food options as part of the Navy’s efforts to improve sailor quality of life.
